teachings.text_english: a day on which people arouse each other to buttress the Torah and Yiddishkeit in every place according to its needs
teachings.passage: In it the Rebbe Rayatz urges that this day be set aside as a day of farbrengen — 'a day on which people arouse each other to buttress the Torah and Yiddishkeit in every place according to its needs.'

teachings.text_english: Why are you crying? When you grow up you will be a chassid and you'll teach Chassidus articulately.
teachings.passage: my grandfather the Rebbe Maharash had said: 'Why are you crying? When you grow up you will be a chassid and you'll teach Chassidus articulately.'

teachings.text_english: We belong to our father's party; we are non-partisan daughters of Israel. We are fond of the old ways of our Patriarch Israel, and detest the new aspirations.
teachings.passage: 'We belong to our father's party,' they replied; 'we are non-partisan daughters of Israel. We are fond of the old ways of our Patriarch Israel, and detest the new aspirations.'

teachings.text_english: What a repulsive and unjustifiable thing it is to take hold of men with minds and opinions by the might of a fist and by the threat of a revolver!
teachings.passage: 'What a repulsive and unjustifiable thing it is to take hold of men with minds and opinions by the might of a fist and by the threat of a revolver!'

teachings.text_english: Only wishful thinking could imagine that tears and pleas could help. There's nothing in common between a cruel gentile and pleas.
teachings.passage: Smiling, I explained to my wife and daughters: 'Only wishful thinking could imagine that tears and pleas could help. There's nothing in common between a cruel gentile and pleas.'

teachings.text_english: If imprisoning a body in a jail of wood and stone is called suffering, then how intense must be the suffering of the Divine soul when it is imprisoned in the body and the animal soul. This is something worth thinking about deeply.
teachings.passage: If imprisoning a body in a jail of wood and stone is called suffering, then how intense must be the suffering of the Divine soul when it is imprisoned in the body and the animal soul.

teachings.text_english: Your grandfather was a chassid, so he had the good fortune to carry my grandfather's parcels wherever my grandfather went; you want to carry this parcel so that I should go (G-d forbid) where you want me to go. No, that cannot be! I'm not going to go your way. You're right: chassidim remain chassidim...!
teachings.passage: Taking it from his hand I said: 'Your grandfather was a chassid, so he had the good fortune to carry my grandfather's parcels wherever my grandfather went; you want to carry this parcel so that I should go (G-d forbid) where you want me to go. No, that cannot be! I'm not going to go your way. You're right: chassidim remain chassidim...!'

events.description: The Rebbe was arrested at home during supper with family by GPU officials
events.passage: I was arrested in my home at 22 Machovaya St., apartment 12. I was having supper in the company of my family and no one else.

events.description: The Rebbe was transported to Spalerka prison in a van
events.passage: We now received our orders: 'Honored citizens, stand up and climb down!'

events.description: The Rebbe entered the large courtyard of Spalerka prison
events.passage: We passed through a large courtyard enclosed on each side by a building of six floors and two or three entrances.

events.description: The Rebbe accidentally turned into the wrong corridor, which he saw as Divine Providence
events.passage: Whether unwittingly or intentionally I do not know, but evidently, because I was immersed in thought, or because of confusion, when I approached that open door I turned into another long corridor which branched off to the right.

events.description: The Rebbe met with prison guard Chimke who knew Lubavitch from childhood
events.passage: The family of a holy man lived near the market place of Lubavitch, in a big courtyard in which there was a well. Every time I visited the market place with my father I used to drink water there

events.description: The Rebbe refused to complete the standard questionnaire and only provided basic information
events.passage: I have nothing to write. Nothing that is written here applies to me, and there's nothing here for me to answer.

events.description: The Rebbe was assigned prisoner number 26818
events.passage: The secretary finished writing in the big books, filled in the number 26818 on the dotted line, and at that moment I became a yarlik.

events.description: The Rebbe's family fled Lubavitch due to approaching German forces
events.passage: In 1915 we fled from the approaching Germans

events.description: The Rebbe's family moved to Leningrad
events.passage: in May of that year we arrived in Leningrad

concepts.definition_short: Teaching that God's providence applies to all details of creation, even inanimate objects
Divine scholarship, the mystical teachings of Chassidism
Phylacteries worn during prayer
Prayer, Jewish liturgical worship
Prisoner identification number that replaces one's name in the Soviet prison system
Hell, metaphorically used to describe the prison experience
Evil spiritual force, metaphorically represented by the leech in the teaching

teachings.text_english: Who can stand and face His indignation, and who can withstand the fierceness of His anger? The leech has two daughters [who cry]: 'Give, give!' Two groups of angels stand at the entrances of Gehinnom and say: 'Give, give! Come, come!'
teachings.source_ref: Reishis Chochmah: Shaar HaYirah 13:13, Nahum 1:6, Prov. 30:15, Avorah Zarah 17a
teachings.passage: At that moment I was reminded that Reishis Chochmah, at the beginning of Maseches Gehinnom, quotes the verse, "Who can stand and face His indignation, and who can withstand the fierceness of His anger?" R. Zeira introduced his [mystical] comment [by quoting another verse]: "The leech [a metaphor for kelipah] has two daughters [who cry]: 'Give, give!'" And R. Elazar said: "Two groups of angels stand at the entrances of Gehinnom and say: 'Give, give! Come, come!'"

teachings.text_english: Divine Providence applies in particularized form to all created things, including inanimate objects, the vegetative kingdom, and the animal kingdom. It is decided on high how many times the leaf or the wisp of straw will roll over, and to what place it will come to rest.
teachings.source_ref: maamar known as Tik'u 5688 (1928), sichah of Yud-Tes Kislev, 5694, HaYom Yom
teachings.passage: as our mentor the Baal Shem Tov teaches, even these subjects are governed by Divine Providence (as is explained in the maamar known as Tik'u 5688 [1928])

teachings.text_english: Give thanks to G-d for He is good!
teachings.passage: "Give thanks to G-d for He is good!" Through His lovingkindness the opportunity arose for me to make a wrong turning into this corridor

teachings.text_english: Even though I am a communist, I will keep my word.
teachings.passage: In fact he concluded sarcastically: 'Even though I am a communist, I will keep my word.' That is what he said, and I now demand that his promise be fulfilled.

teachings.text_english: You have transgressed enough and more than enough against the proletarians, but now you will pay for it all!
teachings.passage: You have transgressed enough and more than enough against the proletarians, but now you will pay for it all!

teachings.text_english: In the middle of one of the maamarim [which I delivered] on the Rosh HaShanah preceding the arrest of 1927, words were uttered concerning the teaching of the Baal Shem Tov as to how Divine Providence applies to every detail even within the inanimate, vegetative, and animal realms — even though this was not relevant to the maamar then being delivered. If not for this, I do not know if I could have managed to bear — and survive — the torment of that imprisonment.
teachings.passage: My revered father-in-law, the Rebbe [Rayatz], once said: "In the middle of one of the maamarim [which I delivered] on the Rosh HaShanah preceding the arrest of 1927, words were uttered concerning the teaching of the Baal Shem Tov...

events.description: Secretary brought the Rebbe with documents (yarlik no. 26818) to the clerks
events.passage: The secretary quickly approached one of them and said, 'I have brought yarlik no. 26818,' and put the documents on the table.

events.passage: No religious garments are allowed inside these prison walls!

events.description: The Rebbe spoke out clearly about his rights as a prisoner and his religious obligations
events.passage: The law allows me to demand my belongings and gives me permission to pray!

events.description: The Rebbe was placed in a red room measuring two meters by four
events.passage: The room was two meters by four and painted red, and had no other door.

events.description: The Rebbe Rashab and his brother traveled through Crimea and discovered a cave for prayer
events.passage: when he had traveled with his brother, my uncle R. Zalman Aharon, in 5644 (1884)

events.description: Family journey when the Rebbe was five or six years old, traveling to Yalta for health reasons
events.passage: At that time, in a covered wagon drawn by four horses, according to local custom, we were traveling among the mountains of Crimea

events.description: Two-week stay for medical tests prescribed for the Rebbe Rashab
events.passage: we stayed in Kharkov for about two weeks for the medical tests which the specialists had prescribed for my father

events.description: The Rebbe was transferred to the third stage of the prison complex
events.passage: The iron gate was thrown open and we entered Stage Three.

events.description: Guard struck the Rebbe and pushed him down iron staircase for attempting to put on tefillin
events.passage: before I managed to put on my head-tefillin he turned around and struck me. In doing so he pushed me down the whole of the iron staircase

events.description: The Rebbe was brought to the chief who was to process his imprisonment
events.passage: The chief of the sixth division stood up there on the landing, waiting to receive his important visitor

events.description: The Rebbe was searched and his belongings confiscated, registered as prisoner number 160, the fourth
events.passage: Your name, old man, is now 'no. 160, the fourth.'

events.description: The Rebbe managed to put on tefillin and recite Shema before being discovered
events.passage: Without losing an instant I quickly put on the hand-tefillin then the head-tefillin and recited Kerias Shema

events.description: The Rebbe wrote three telegrams requesting his tefillin be returned
events.passage: I wrote out three identical telegrams: 'I hereby request that the chief of the sixth division be ordered immediately to hand me my tefillin. Rabbi Y. Schneersohn, sixth division, room 160.'

events.description: The Rebbe was violently pushed into his assigned cell
events.passage: Here's your lock-up. Get in there and lie down on the floor.

Mystical concept of spiritual influence upon material places
Jewish self-respect and dignity that must not be trampled
Meditation upon G-d's absolute unity filling the entire universe

teachings.text_english: I am neither able nor permitted to think such thoughts that lower my spirits. There is no room here for being melancholy or downcast. The place and the time demand G-d's lovingkindness and help in fortifying one's heart and raising one's spirits and focusing one's mind and heart on the detailed workings of Divine Providence.
teachings.passage: I said to myself: 'I am neither able nor permitted to think such thoughts that lower my spirits...'

teachings.text_english: every word or syllable, every sight or image, that a man hears or sees, is a directive in some area of his divine service
teachings.passage: We all know the Baal Shem Tov's teaching — that every word or syllable, every sight or image, that a man hears or sees, is a directive in some area of his divine service.

teachings.text_english: G-d created the world in a way that allows every Jew to fulfill His commandments. Since a Jew may be traveling away from home and it is time to daven, but one may not pray out in an open field, G-d created caves such as this in which he can pray.
teachings.passage: My father now explained that G-d created the world in a way that allows every Jew to fulfill His commandments

teachings.text_english: The footsteps of upright men are like a Dew of Resurrection upon the stone and wooden floors of these dismal passages and upon these iron staircases, which were built only in order to visit suffering and distress, anguish and oppression on all those who ever trod them.
teachings.passage: The footsteps of upright men are like a Dew of Resurrection upon the stone and wooden floors

teachings.text_english: Bourgeois practices?! So now we should give them servants?! Are the prisoners too weak to carry their own bags?! No religious garments are allowed inside these prison walls!
teachings.passage: Bourgeois practices?! he fumed. So now we should give them servants?!

teachings.text_english: In the course of my two hours here I've heard dozens of times that I am a prisoner. I don't know whether I alone am a prisoner, or whether all the officials here are prisoners too. Like me, you too are not allowed to budge from your allotted positions. Just as I am obliged to obey orders, you too are obligated to fulfill your duty. Stop making scornful declarations about every religious matter that is holy to me. The law allows me to demand my belongings and gives me permission to pray!
teachings.passage: My reply was impassioned: 'In the course of my two hours here I've heard dozens of times that I am a prisoner...'

teachings.text_english: Look upon the afflictions of Your people! It is not me that they have imprisoned, nor me that they want to punish. For what am I? It is only that I descend from holy forebears, and am one of the stones which underlie the pillars of Your courtyards, the courtyards of the Torah. G-d, I entreat You!
teachings.passage: I entreat You, G-d: Look upon the afflictions of Your people!

concept_developments.description: Applied the concept of Divine Providence to finding meaning and strength during imprisonment

teachings.text_english: From where did Moshe Rabbeinu know when it was day and when it was night? — When he heard the ministering angels saying Kadosh... — 'Holy [...is the G-d of Hosts],' he knew that it was day, and when he heard them saying Baruch... — 'Blessed [be the glory of G-d...],' he knew that it was night.
teachings.passage: The Midrash teaches: "From where did Moshe Rabbeinu know when it was day and when it was night?"

teachings.text_english: If my grandfather had lived in the time of the tannaim he would have been a great tanna; if he had lived in the time of the prophets he would have been a great prophet. My grandfather was a tanna and a prophet, but because the generation was unworthy this was hidden except for those who were worthy.
teachings.passage: My greatgrandfather the Tzemach Tzedek said of him (this I heard from my saintly grandmother, Rebbitzin Rivkah)

teachings.text_english: The teaching of the Sages — 'Whoever says I have nothing but Torah study does not have even Torah' — caused me to take the path to Mezritch.
teachings.passage: The Alter Rebbe once told his son, the Mitteler Rebbe

teachings.text_english: I do know something about how to study, but I do not know how to serve G-d in prayer. Let me therefore first go to Mezritch to see what path they follow, and from there I will continue to Vilna.
teachings.passage: In Vilna they teach you how to study; in Mezritch they teach you how to pray

teachings.text_english: Chassidus is a remedy for the glimmer of the soul which is garbed in the body, that it revives the soul from its state of faint languor, arouses it from its slumbers, and cleanses and purifies the body of its uncleanliness.
teachings.passage: Everyone knew that Chassidus is a remedy for the glimmer of the soul

teachings.text_english: Divinity is everything; Everything is Divinity.
teachings.passage: The Baal Shem Tov's school of thought is basically one teaching with two aspects

teachings.text_english: Man's main goal is not Torah study exclusively, but Torah study when joined with deeds and with the service of the heart through prayer — for whoever says 'I have nothing but Torah study alone,' does not have even true Torah study.
teachings.passage: There is another major school of thought in divine service

teachings.text_english: In the days of the Tzemach Tzedek, at almost all times through the year there were venerable and hoary chassidim visiting Lubavitch. So there were always opportunities to be of service to great men, to observe meanwhile how they davened and how they ate, how they studied and how they conducted themselves. Sometimes this affected young scholars even more than their actual studies.
teachings.passage: As R. Shmuel recalled as we traveled

teachings.text_english: Being of service to Torah sages is superior [even] to the study of the Torah itself.
teachings.passage: As the Talmud teaches

teachings.text_english: Our bodies alone were banished into exile to be ruled by the nations of the world. Our souls were never exiled, nor were they subjected to the rule of the nations.
teachings.passage: This is reflected in the well-known statement of one of our holy forebears

teachings.text_english: We must openly declare for all to hear, that with regard to everything involving our religion — the Torah of the people of Israel, with its commandments and customs — no one is going to impose his views on us, and no force has the right to subjugate us.
teachings.passage: statement of one of our holy forebears

teachings.text_english: With all the power of Jewish stubbornness and with our thousand-year heritage of mesirus nefesh, we must say, 'Do not touch My anointed ones, and do not harm My prophets.'
teachings.passage: statement of one of our holy forebears

teachings.text_english: May the L-rd our G-d be with us as He was with our fathers; may He not forsake us, nor abandon us.
teachings.passage: We ask G-d, blessed be He

teachings.text_english: We must always bear in mind that prisons and hard-labor camps are transient, whereas the Torah, its mitzvos, and the Jewish people, are eternal.

teachings.text_english: Your father's father was a Rebbe and a great scholar, who studied Torah and davened all his days. He instructed his sons — your uncle R. Zalman Aharon, your father, and your uncle R. Menachem Mendel — never to engage in any business, but only to sit and study.

teachings.text_english: There is no salvation for the Jews on any road other than Communism
I have already declared that I will not abandon my principles. No man or demon has been born, nor will be born that will make me budge even slightly

Better for the 'Yevseks' to fight Yiddishkeit than to entrust the process to the tender mercies of the Russians; The danger is that the masses may think that Judaism is exempt from anti-religious propaganda and, therefore, it rests with the Jewish Communists to be even more ruthless with rabbis than non-Jewish Communists are with priests
Your destroyers and they that made you waste shall go forth from you
We have been left with nothing ...we have no G-d and we have no Torah. All we have left are the letters of the Yiddish alphabet
The G.P.U. is aware of Rabbi Schneersohn's crime, and we have decided to uproot him. We already have a collection of the necessary evidence to accomplish this
After extensive investigation we have discovered that the Lubavitcher Rebbe was the primary cause of all these activities and that his agents were involved in all of them
We are fully knowledgeable of his actions. We will tear him from his roots and have already commenced gathering the necessary evidence

I do not know which Schneersohn you seek. If you enter into someone's home, surely you know in advance who dwells there, and this drama is pointless. Deliver your message and clearly state your wishes

I am not fully aware of your methods, and I have no desire to know them. Either you are completely in error, or someone has fabricated a libel against us. In any event, it makes no difference to me. As for the emissaries from your organization, I have not feared, I do not fear, and I will not fear them

teachings.text_english: Why? this we are not obligated to answer you. You asked regarding our beliefs and I replied. As to the question 'why' this we are not obligated to explain
teachings.passage: Why?' replied Shaina, 'this we are not obligated to answer you.You asked regarding our beliefs and I replied. As to the question 'why' this we are not obligated to explain'

teachings.text_english: The G.P.U. can force even the silent tongue to speak and tell what is hidden within the heart. Our interrogators are remarkable craftsmen. To them all is revealed, willingly or otherwise
teachings.passage: The G.P.U. can force even the silent tongue to speak and tell what is hidden within the heart. Our interrogators are remarkable craftsmen. To them all is revealed, willingly or otherwise

teachings.text_english: The entire tragedy is that you wish to accomplish everything by power and coercion. This is unethical and repugnant, attempting to intimidate intelligent and informed people with the power of the fist and the threats of the gun
teachings.passage: The entire tragedy' answered my daughter, 'is that you wish to accomplish everything by power and coercion. This is unethical and repugnant'

teachings.text_english: I am involved in the promulgation of Torah and its mitzvot! I will assume the entire responsibility and incriminate no one else
teachings.passage: But I will only teU them: 'I am involved in the promulgation of Torah and its mitzvot! I will assume the entire responsibility and incriminate no one else'

teachings.text_english: Chassidim remain chassidim
teachings.occasion: offering to carry the Rebbe's bag
teachings.passage: Give the bag to me, I will carry it. Chassidim remain chassidim! my grandfather carried the bags of your grandfather

teachings.text_english: Your grandfather was a chassid of my grandfather, so he was privileged to carry those bags to the place of my grandfathers choice, whereas you desire to carry my bag, G-d forbid, to a destination of your choosing. No, this cannot be. In your way I will not go. Chassidim certainly do remain chassidim!
teachings.passage: Your grandfather was a chassid of my grandfather, so he was privileged to carry those bags to the place of my grandfathers choice, whereas you desire to carry my bag, G-d forbid, to a destination of your choosing

teachings.text_english: Do you forget where you are? Or is your mind confused? Do you mean to institute new procedures in this department?
teachings.passage: Do you forget where you are? Or is your mind confused?

teachings.text_english: I know very well that I am a prisoner brought to Spalerno, and my mind, thank G-d, is in good order. I do not seek to institute new procedures. My name is Schneersohn. I live at 22 Machovaya Street, Apartment 12. I will not write any answers to the questionnaire, and you can enter the information just given.
teachings.passage: I know very well that I am a prisoner brought to Spalerno

teachings.text_english: I am an 'Honored-for-Generations-Citizen.'
teachings.passage: I am an'Honored-for-Generations-Citizen.'

teachings.text_english: I am involved in studies, the study of the knowledge of G-dliness known as Chassidus, and the study of Jewish Law and its observance in accordance with the Jewish religion.
teachings.passage: I am involved in studies, the study of the knowledge of G-dliness known as Chassidus

teachings.text_english: Yes! The knowledge of G-dliness. One G-d has created and formed all existence, and His Divine Providence extends over all creations: the crawling creature in the sea and the small creatures in a desert wasteland, and mankind in civilized society.
teachings.passage: One G-d has created and formed all existence

teachings.text_english: Who compels you to write? As far as I am concerned, you need not write anything. If you want to write, then write, and if you don't want to, then don't.
teachings.passage: Who compels you to write?

teachings.text_english: Though I am a Communist—I will not lie.
teachings.passage: His final words to me were 'Though I am a Communist'—this was with irony—'I will not lie.'

teachings.text_english: G-d created the world so as to enable each Jew's fulfillment of G-d's commands. Thus, a traveling Jew may realize that it is time for prayer. According to Jewish Law, he should not pray in an open field, but in an enclosed area. It was for this very reason that G-d created rocks formed in this fashion, similar to the shelter of a house, so that a Jew could pray in a proper way even in this remote area.
teachings.passage: my father explained to me that G-d created the world so as to enable each Jew's fulfillment of G-d's commands

teachings.text_english: The two yuds correspond to the Inclinations, the Virtuous and Evil. Both the Evil and Virtuous Inclinations contain the ten qualities of the soul. There are two kinds of yuds: the yud of the Virtuous Inclination and the yud of the Evil Inclination. Haman's decree was not directed specifically against G-d-fearing Jews, those of the Virtuous Inclination; he sought also to destroy those who were irreligious, who acted in accord with the Evil Inclination. They, too, were included in his edict.
teachings.passage: The Tzemach Tzedek replied:'The two yuds correspond to the Inclinations, the Virtuous and Evil

teachings.text_english: The same is true of you; when you will suffer greatly, then you, too, will change greatly
teachings.passage: The Tzemach Tzedek concluded: 'The same is true of you; when you will suffer greatly, then you, too, will change greatly'

teachings.text_english: When you will also suffer, you, too, will change.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe concluded his narrative and then added, 'When you will also suffer, you, too, will change.'

teachings.text_english: Do you see this arm extended before you? From the time I was fourteen years old it has been engaged in the sacred task of annihilating those, such as yourself, who oppose enlightenment. We will destroy them all. You request talit and tefillin? We will cast them into the refuse.
teachings.passage: Do you see this arm extended before you? From the time I was fourteen years old it has been engaged in the sacred task of annihilating those

teachings.text_english: Vile creature!
teachings.passage: The Rebbe pounded on the table and cried out: 'Vile creature!'

teachings.text_english: This is the first time that I have come into a room and not a single person has arisen from his place!
teachings.passage: As the Rebbe entered, he turned to the interrogators and commented: 'This is the first time that I have come into a room and not a single person has arisen from his place!'

teachings.text_english: Most assuredly, I am in a place that is not obligated to have a mezuzah. There are various such places which are not required to have one, as, for example, a stable or a bathroom.

events.description: Professor Baratchenko began correspondence with the Rebbe seeking esoteric meaning of the Magen David symbol

events.title_en: Rebbe wrote letter supporting agricultural settlement
events.description: The Rebbe wrote a letter to American Jews urging support for Jewish agricultural settlement in Soviet Union
events.passage: Three years ago, in the year 1924, I wrote a letter to the Jews of America urging them to support the Jewish agricultural settlement in the Soviet Union

events.description: First interrogation concluded late at night with Lulav threatening the Rebbe would be shot in twenty-four hours
events.passage: The inquiry concluded late at night; at the end Lulav, who had a tendency to stammer, blurted out in anger; 'B-B-B-ut in another twenty-four hours you will be shot!'

events.title_en: Tefillin and sacred books brought to Rebbe
events.description: A Jewish member of the G.P.U. brought the Rebbe his tefillin and sacred books on Friday afternoon
events.passage: Finally, on Friday afternoon a Jewish member of the G.P.U. brought him his tefillin and sacred books

events.description: The Rebbe's first Sabbath meal in prison consisted of challah and cold water, reciting Kiddush and blessing on bread with Chabad melody
events.passage: The Rebbe's first Shabhat meal in prison consisted of a challah and some cold water from the faucet. He recited Kiddush and made the blessing on bread out loud, chanting them with a particular Chabad melody

events.description: Multiple attempts to photograph the Rebbe, finally successful after Sabbath with Rebbe wearing yarmulke and displaying tzitzit
events.passage: About four o'clock Shabhat afternoon they came to photograph the Rebbe

events.description: Seven o'clock morning meeting at Rebbe's home, moved to Jewish community center by ten o'clock with representatives from every party except communists
events.passage: Seven o'clock on the morning of the arrest, a meeting was held at the Rebbe's home. By ten o'clock the meeting moved to a Jewish community center

events.description: Religious Jew from Moscow brought ominous news that G.P.U. had decided to execute the Rebbe
events.passage: On Shabbat morning, a religious Jew from Moscow arrived with the ominous news. The G.P.U. had decided to execute the Rebbe

events.description: Several hundred thousand Jews across the USSR signed petitions begging for the Rebbe's release
events.passage: Several hundred thousand Jews across the U.S.S.R. signed petitions begging for the Rebbe's release

events.description: Monday and Thursday of first week declared fast days in communities across USSR, described as observing Yom Kippur twice
events.passage: Monday and Thursday of the first week were declared fast days in communities across the U.S.S.R. Russian Jewry observed Yom Kippur twice that week

events.description: Madam Peshkova notified the committee that the death sentence had been stayed
events.passage: Finally, Madam Peshkova notified the committee; the death sentence had been stayed

events.description: Rebbe taken for second interrogation on Tuesday evening, asked again about Chassidus and subjected to same accusations
events.passage: On Tuesday evening, the 21st of Sivan (June 21), the Rebbe was taken for a second interrogation

events.description: Rebbe was supposed to depart for Solovaki Islands, family came to see him off, huge crowd gathered outside
events.passage: On Wednesday, 29 Sivan (June 29) the Rebbe was supposed to depart for the Solovaki Islands of Siberia

events.description: While crowd waited outside, Rebbe called for third interrogation, offered to be freed if he would support rabbinical conference
events.passage: While the crowd waited outside, the Rebbe was being called for his third interrogation

events.title_en: Rebbe beaten for refusing to stand
events.description: Rebbe repeatedly beaten by guards for refusing to stand when receiving information, suffered lasting pain from blow under chin
events.passage: Thursday, Rosh Chodesh Tammuz, (June 30) eleven o'clock in the morning, a guard entered the Rebbe's cell and commanded him to stand

events.title_en: Sentence commuted to exile in Kostroma
events.description: Rebbe informed his sentence reduced to three years exile in Kostroma, observed documents showing crossed-out death sentence
events.passage: There he was informed that his sentence had been reduced, as Lulav had mentioned. He was to be freed from prison and sent for three years exile to the city of Kostroma

events.description: After nineteen days imprisonment, Rebbe returned home for six hours before departure to Kostroma
events.passage: On the afternoon of Sunday, the third of Tammuz, (July 3), after nineteen days of imprisonment in Spalerno, the Rebbe was called to the prison office

events.description: Rebbe delivered inspiring speech from train coach to large gathering before departure to Kostroma
events.passage: From the step of the coach, the Rebbe turned to the large gathering and spoke these inspiring words

events.description: Train arrived late at night, Reb Michoel Dworkin had prepared cheder, mikvah, and lodging in advance
events.passage: The train arrived late at night. According to instructions, the Rebbe was to report to the local authorities immediately upon arrival

events.description: Rebbe reported to local G.P.U. authorities, given restrictions and requirements as exile-prisoner
events.passage: On Tuesday, the 5th day of Tammuz, the Rebbe came to the G.P.U. authorities of Kostroma

events.description: On Rebbe's birthday, local G.P.U. official informed him of complete amnesty and full freedom
events.passage: On Tuesday, the 12th of Tammuz (July 12), coinciding with the Rebbe's birthday in 5640 (1880), the Rebbe appeared at the headquarters of the G.P.U.

events.title_en: Maamar 'G-d is among those that help me'
events.description: Rebbe delivered chassidic discourse beginning 'G-d is among those that help me' on day of liberation
events.passage: On that very day, the 12th of Tammuz, a large gathering of Jews assembled in his lodging in Kostroma and he delivered the ma'amar beginning: 'G-d is among those that help me.'

events.title_en: Maamar 'Blessed be the One Who bestows good'
events.description: After receiving Certificate of Release, Rebbe delivered second maamar
events.passage: After receiving the Certificate of Release, the Rebbe delivered the ma'amar 'Blessed be the One Who bestows good upon the unworthy who has bestowed good upon me'

events.description: Rebbe left Kostroma as free citizen at nine in morning accompanied by two emissaries from Jewish community
events.passage: On the 14th of Tammuz (July 14), at nine in the morning, the Rebbe left the city of Kostroma a free citizen

events.description: Rebbe arrived home in Leningrad on Friday
events.passage: on Friday the 16th he arrived in Leningrad accompanied by two emissaries especially chosen by the Jewish community of Kostroma

events.description: Rebbe called to Torah, recited Hagomel blessing, delivered multiple maamarim at kiddush and Sabbath meal
events.passage: On the Shabbat following his return to Leningrad, the Rebbe was called up to the Torah (Parshat Pinchas), and recited the benediction of Hagomel

events.description: Special meeting held at Soviet Foreign Office with Rebbe's departure as only agenda item
events.passage: The many intense efforts on the Rebbe's behalf finally resulted in a special meeting held at the Soviet Foreign Office on the second day of Rosh Hashanah 5688 (1927)

events.description: Rebbe left Russia with family, six Chassidim, library and possessions in four train cars
events.passage: On the day after Sukkot 5688 (1927), the Rebbe left Russia, accompanied by his entire family and six Chassidim

events.description: Reb Simcha and 15 others arrested after Bucharian Jews commemorated Rebbe's liberation, public trial held with Tanya and Rebbe's picture as 'weapons'
events.passage: On the 12th of Tammuz, 5704 (1944), Bucharian Jews gathered in Tashkent to commemorate the liberation of the Rebbe

teachings.text_english: I will not deny that Jews think of me as an authority, but I am not guilty of any crime. I do not compel or assert my authority over anyone. I have never used my authority in any way against the Soviet Union. The charge that I rule over Jews is wholly incorrect. Coercion and force are wholly alien to the ways of Chassidus. Leadership, from a Chassidic viewpoint, means striving to achieve moral refinement and spiritual perfection, so that others may learn to emulate and proceed in these same paths. This cannot be achieved by force or power—only by means of a free will. Chassidim, of their free volition, emulate their Rebbe and leader.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe replied patiently

teachings.text_english: Give heed to me, Lulav, you desire to accuse me with a new Beilis accusation. Remember how Czar Nicholas engaged professors and scholars in order to direct a blood libel against the Jews and was discredited? You will be frustrated in your intentions.
teachings.passage: the Rebbe directed in a firm voice

teachings.text_english: If you compel me to remove my talit katan, I will refuse to answer your questions. And if you intend to achieve this by means of fists and guns, I challenge you to do so.
teachings.passage: replied the Rebbe

teachings.text_english: Lulav, do you know that my parents were childless, and only after my father went to the Rebbe of Lubavitch did they give birth to a son—and that son is Nachmanson who stands before you.
teachings.passage: Nachmanson smiled and said

teachings.text_english: B-B-B-ut in another twenty-four hours you will be shot!
teachings.passage: Lulav, who had a tendency to stammer, blurted out in anger

teachings.text_english: I will not eat the prison food—only that which is brought from my home. As for water, I will accept it later from the prison, but only on the condition that it is warmed up in a vessel used only for water.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe told him

teachings.text_english: Several of you will be sent to Solovaki and several will be freed. Wherever you go, you must relate that you were in Spalerka and that you saw the Lubavitcher Rebbe, who was arrested for establishing chadorim, yeshivot and mikva'ot. No one should be hindered from these activities, and the work must continue.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe told them

teachings.text_english: We raise our lips in prayer to G-d: 'Yehi Hashem Elokeinu imanu ka'asher haya im avoteinu. Al ya'azveinu ve'al yitsheinu!' 'May G-d be with us as He was with our ancestors, to neither forsake nor abandon us.'—And He will in fact be with us, though our merit is not comparable to that of our ancestors, who endured intense self-sacrifice for the sake of Torah and its mitzvot.
teachings.passage: From the step of the coach, the Rebbe turned to the large gathering and spoke these inspiring words

teachings.text_english: Only our bodies were sent into exile and subjugated to alien rule; our souls were not given over into captivity and foreign rule. We must proclaim openly and before all, that any matter affecting the Jewish religion, Torah, and its mitzvot and customs is not subject to the coercion of others.
teachings.passage: From the step of the coach, the Rebbe turned to the large gathering and spoke these inspiring words

teachings.text_english: If I were to be offered a million dollars to again experience one moment of my past anguish, I would decline. And if someone would advance me a million dollars to erase a moment of past suffering—I would also refuse.
teachings.passage: he was asked for his reaction to his many recent experiences, and he answered

teachings.text_english: Nyet, nyet nikavo—Nothing exists aside from G-d!
teachings.passage: the chassid Reb Michoel Dworkin was dancing round the house, a bottle of wine in his hand, and upon his lips a melody with Russian words, singing with great feeling

teachings.text_english: I do not regret anything. You can threaten me with the worst punishments. You will not achieve your aims. You can send me to Siberia for it, but I will not deviate from my ways.
teachings.passage: the Rebbe answered

teachings.text_english: We shall see who will teach whom.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe replied in Yiddish

events.description: Liberation after imprisonment, followed by chassidic discourse
events.passage: The following chassidic discourse (Kostroma, 13th of Tammuz, 5687 / July 13, 1927) is one of three delivered by the Rebbe in Kostroma after his liberation from Soviet prison

events.description: First of seven imprisonments when Rebbe was 11 years old
events.passage: The first imprisonment was in Lubavitch when I was 11 years old. This was the time—5652 (1892)

events.description: Due to slander by haskalah school teachers
events.passage: The second imprisonment was in Lubavitch in Iyar 5662 (1902) due to slander spread by school teachers, members of the haskalah movement

events.description: Due to Labor Zionist party uprising against police
events.passage: The third imprisonment was in Lubavitch in Tevet 5666 (1906) due to the participation of members of the Labor Zionist party in an uprising against the police

events.description: Due to slander by Jewish scholar K.
events.passage: The fourth imprisonment was in Petersburg in Tevet 5670 (1910) due to the slander of the Jewish scholar K.

events.description: Due to efforts obtaining military exemptions for religious officials
events.passage: The fifth imprisonment was in Petersburg in Shevat 5676 (1916) due to my efforts in obtaining materials about the regulations and laws exempting religious officials from military service

events.description: Due to slander by head of local Yevsektzia
events.passage: The sixth imprisonment was in Tammuz 5680 (1920) in Rostov-on-Don, due to slander by the head of the Yevsektzia there

events.description: Most significant imprisonment, lasted 18 days, 11 hours, 15 minutes
events.passage: The imprisonment began at 2:15 at night, Tuesday night before Wednesday 15th of Sivan 5687 (1927) and continued until 1:30 in the afternoon on Sunday, the 3rd of Tammuz

events.description: Liberation declared a festival for those promoting Torah
events.passage: And [last year] on the twelfth day of Tammuz, corresponding to the Torah reading 'I herewith give you my covenant of peace,' I was freed

events.description: Compelled to go into exile for three years
events.passage: On that day I was compelled to go into exile to the city of banishment, Kostroma and to be there for the term of three years

events.description: Arrived Monday after liberation
events.passage: I came there the next day, on Monday, the 4th of Tammuz

events.description: Final release from exile
events.passage: I was kept in exile until Wednesday 13th of Tammuz, 12:30 in the afternoon

events.description: 11-year-old Rebbe imprisoned for defending fellow Jew
events.passage: When he met us, his dark face lit up and his teeth gleamed in a radiant smile, as he said: 'With G-d's help, I hope to make a good profit.' As he spoke, one of the minor police officials approached with quick strides and struck Reb Dovid with such force that blood flowed from his nose. I immediately shouted at the officer, 'You wretched drunkard!' and pushed him forcefully

events.description: Calf found in prison cell, leading to guard's conviction
events.passage: After an extended investigation, it was discovered that both the guard and his brother had stolen the calf from Reb Meyer

events.title_en: Rebbe's mission to nullify Stolypin's decree
events.description: Rebbe spent Shabbat in tavern to meet official and nullify anti-Jewish decree
events.passage: After many efforts, an audience was arranged for a Friday evening. Since Pobedonostsev lived in a remote suburb of Petersburg, the Rebbe had to leave Petersburg early and spend the entire Shabbat in that area

events.description: Trip to prepare for thirteenth meeting
events.passage: In about an hour and a half, at 9:15, I am to take the express train to Moscow. The purpose of the trip is to make all necessary preparations for the thirteenth meeting

events.title_en: Three recitations of ma'amar Ve'atah Tetzaveh
events.description: Chassidic discourse recited three times over Shabbat
events.passage: It occurred to me this time to recite the ma'amar on Friday night before Kabbalat Shabbat, to review it on Shabbat morning, and to say it a third time at the normal time for reviewing it

events.title_en: Meeting with Mark Bashkov on train
events.description: Encounter with U.G.P.O. member from chassidic family
events.passage: The man entered and said, 'My name is Mark Saminovitch Bashkov.' He was the chairman of the Sovnorkom in the city of Tsheliabinsky, and a member of the general U.G.P.O.

events.description: Wagon driver's war service following Rebbe's father's instructions
events.passage: A full year, related Yaakov Lifshitz, he was in the front lines. Bullets and death flew from all sides and he was never injured

events.description: Rebbe forced to change hotels to avoid Yevsektzia
events.passage: a quarter hour earlier a man had come to the hotel office asking if Rabbi Schneersohn had already arrived, and he explained that he was a member of the Yevsektzia

events.description: Meeting to check financial records and discuss proposals
events.passage: The meeting began at 12:15 p.m. and lasted until 4:30. We checked the financial records and discussed the proposals

events.description: Major funding meeting securing 60,000 for vocational work and 40,000 for religious institutions
events.passage: We came at the appointed time, 6:00, the Doctor came out to receive us, and I saw that he was in good spirits

events.description: Yevsektzia leader's speech targeting Rabbi Schneersohn's activities
events.passage: he told me that the leader of the Yevsektzia, Litvakov, delivered an address describing his opposition to religious activism. He singled out Rabbi Schneersohn in particular

events.passage: Two weeks later a young emissary arrived who spoke Russian fluently. He was accompanied by an associate who spoke the Georgian dialect. They presented themselves as emissaries from the Central Committee who had come to restore the mikveh

events.description: Synagogue and mikveh restored with funding from Rebbe's organization
events.passage: There too, after Shavelov had closed the synagogue and stopped up the mikveh, young men arrived speaking in the name of the Central Committee

teachings.text_english: Before its descent, the soul is made to swear, 'You shall be a righteous person and not be a wicked person!'
teachings.passage: Our Sages of blessed memory state: 'Before its descent, the soul is made to swear, 'You shall be a righteous person and not be a wicked person!''

teachings.text_english: The evil spirit of man attempts to prevail upon him every day, desiring to slay him
teachings.passage: Our Sages of blessed memory identify our major enemy in the battle to be righteous: 'The evil spirit of man attempts to prevail upon him every day, desiring to slay him'

teachings.text_english: I have created the evil inclination, and I have created Torah as an antidote
teachings.passage: Our Sages offer the following advice to aid in the struggle against evil: 'I have created the evil inclination, and I have created Torah as an antidote'

teachings.text_english: Good is none other than Torah
teachings.passage: for our Sages declared, 'Good is none other than Torah'

teachings.text_english: And the Jews fulfilled and received
teachings.passage: It is written, 'And the Jews fulfilled and received,' which our Sages, of blessed memory, explain to mean that the Jews fulfilled to an even greater extent that which they had already received at Sinai in the past

teachings.text_english: I, G-d, have not changed
teachings.passage: The prophet declares 'I, G-d, have not changed.'

teachings.text_english: Dark clouds are descending upon Russia. Twenty two years definitely. The [Alter] Rebbe said that a regime that persecutes the Jewish religion and forbids the study of Torah must meet its destruction eventually
teachings.passage: I remembered the ominous words of my father, the Rebbe, about the new regime, and about the three leaders of the regime—this was about three weeks before his passing

teachings.text_english: Yosef Yitzchak, for disseminating Torah with fear of heaven and strengthening Judaism you must have not only potential but actual self-sacrifice
teachings.passage: Yosef Yitzchak, for disseminating Torah with fear of heaven and strengthening Judaism you must have not only potential but actual self-sacrifice

teachings.text_english: Why are you crying? 'When the month of Adar begins, we increase in joy.' If your work is very hard upon you, remember my instruction to sacrifice yourself for disseminating Torah study with fear of heaven, and for strengthening Judaism, actually, not only in potential
teachings.passage: When I entered, he placed a red silk handkerchief on the open book... His holy face was very serious, and he told me: 'Why are you crying? 'When the month of Adar begins, we increase in joy.''

teachings.text_english: Chai chai yoducha—The living, the living, shall thank You. Two times chai equals 36
teachings.passage: the Rebbe told him: 'Chai chat yoducha—The living, the living, shall thank You' 'Two times chai equals 36.'

teachings.text_english: Be scrupulous not to eat non-kosher food and put on tefillin every weekday. You should also resolve not to violate Shabbat... If you will fulfill these three things, G-d will watch over you so that no bullet shall find you, and He will bring you home in good health
teachings.passage: When he went in for an audience, my father told him, 'Be scrupulous not to eat non-kosher food and put on tefillin every weekday. You should also resolve not to violate Shabbat, for when one violates Shabbat during the war it is not considered a violation except when done deliberately. If you will fulfill these three things, G-d will watch over you so that no bullet shall find you, and He will bring you home in good health.'

teachings.text_english: All Jews are 'Yidden' what kind of a title is that?... So it is, every Jew is a Jew, and that alone is their true title. It is never lost or replaced by others... a Jew, although he has sinned, is still a Jew
teachings.passage: Me: Yid. He: All Jews are 'Yidden' what kind of a title is that? Me: So it is, every Jew is a Jew, and that alone is their true title

teachings.text_english: You have acted in a praiseworthy manner. And if you endured suffering for a few hours for a fellow Jew—then what of it? You have also discovered the importance of knowing Mishnah by heart
teachings.passage: My father at that time said to me: 'You have acted in a praiseworthy manner. And if you endured suffering for a few hours for a fellow Jew—then what of it? You have also discovered the importance of knowing Mishnah by heart'

teachings.text_english: You must respect every Jew who conducts himself with honesty, without distinction as to his being a greater or lesser Torah scholar. And it is necessary to defend the honor of a Jew, even if it entails danger
teachings.passage: The words of my father then are still hewn upon my mind and heart: 'You must respect every Jew who conducts himself with honesty, without distinction as to his being a greater or lesser Torah scholar. And it is necessary to defend the honor of a Jew, even if it entails danger'

teachings.passage: Whip the horses until they cease to be horses

teachings.passage: One should whip the horses until they know they are horses

teachings.text_english: Before 5687 (1927) I was very afraid. I did not speculate about what my own fate would be. I was thinking about chassidim!
teachings.passage: Before 5687 (1927) I was very afraid

teachings.text_english: You should await with caution the end of the year 5687 (1927)
teachings.passage: his father of blessed memory had told him, 'You should await with caution the end of the year 5687 (1927)'

teachings.text_english: A chapter of Psalms will not cause any harm: say a chapter of Psalms for me
teachings.passage: A chapter of Psalms will not cause any harm: say a chapter of Psalms for me

teachings.text_english: I am involved with matters that do not pertain to me. My role is to study and teach Chassidus; this is what I was appointed for
teachings.passage: I am involved with matters that do not pertain to me

teachings.text_english: I desire only peace and brotherhood
teachings.passage: He desired only peace and brotherhood

teachings.passage: I am categorically opposed to calling any gathering

teachings.passage: By nature I trust everyone, and while you say that this is with good intent

teachings.passage: Chassidus has no relation whatsoever to foretelling the future

Explained two paths: preparing to be 'Man' versus elevating to level of 'not a man'

persons.description: Governor of the Besravia region who was the previous tenant of the Rebbe's dwelling on Machovaya 22. Signed over the dwelling and furniture to the Rebbe in his own handwriting before being banished to remote cities of exile in 1924.
Officer of the Opravlenia Doma (Housing Authority) who was falsely accused of stealing 4,000 gold rubles. Was a drunkard and simple-minded person who was blamed by the bookkeeper for the theft.
Evil, scheming person who actually stole the missing money from the Opravlenia Doma and blamed it on the Oprovdom. Tried to implicate the Rebbe by claiming the money was owed by him.

Simple Communist man who was formerly a baker, became director of a large factory. Was prominent among the Cheka and rumored to remain secretly connected to them. Defended the Rebbe's innocence against the false accusations.

Highly honest but volatile individual in charge of paying the Rebbe's rent and dealing with the landlord. Stormed into the office to defend against false accusations and demanded proper investigation.
Rabbi who read the Rebbe's telegram at the Karastien conference when the entire assemblage stood up.
Rabbi who was vacationing in Tzara Sela and failed to heed the Rebbe's warnings about the General Assembly. The Rebbe sent delegations to try to dissuade him from his path.

The Rebbe's son-in-law who was sent with three rabbis to Rabbi Katzenelenbogen. Was present during the arrest and asked the Rebbe about publicity. Went to Moscow to function through the community there.
The Rebbe's daughter who was alone in a room during the arrest and called out the window to Menachem Mendel Schneerson that 'guests have come to visit us!'
Chassid who understood Moussia's coded message about the arrest and immediately ran to inform the close chassidim. Helped wake up others and destroy incriminating documents.
Chassid to whom the Rebbe spoke at the Purim feast, telling him to write harsh letters to all cities and villages warning against sending children to Yevsektzia schools.
The Rebbe's elderly mother who was asked by chassidim to try to calm the Rebbe during the intense Purim feast. Found standing by the Holy Ark calling out to G-d after the arrest.

Chassid to whom the Rebbe spoke at the Purim feast about the care needed to protect children from Yevsektzia schools, telling him to cast himself into flames rather than give children to their schools.
The Rebbe's secretary who possessed many documents pertaining to chadarim and yeshivot throughout the country, as well as financial records. Was awakened to destroy documents after the arrest.
Person the Rebbe said should be met with regarding publicity after his arrest.
One of the two people put in charge of decisions after the Rebbe's arrest, along with R.F.G.
One of the two people put in charge of decisions after the Rebbe's arrest, along with R.M.E.
Chassid who was awakened and informed about the Rebbe's arrest, then proceeded with the author toward the Rebbe's house.
The 'chairman' and arch-zealot promoting the Leningrad Conference who endeavored covertly to thwart the Rebbe's goals while acting benevolently in direct dealings.
Community member who participated in the strategy meeting at Rabbi Katzenelenbogen's home in the resort town of Diska.
Community member who participated in the strategy meeting at Rabbi Katzenelenbogen's home in the resort town of Diska.
German Secretary of State who was entreated to pressure Moscow for the Rebbe's release and led efforts through Ambassador Krestinsky.
German Ambassador who notified Moscow that the Rebbe's arrest had generated agitation all over the globe and advised immediate release.
Rabbi from Vitebsk who came to aid the Moscow community and remained in Moscow the entire period until the first release was obtained.
Rabbi from Minsk who remained in Moscow the entire period until the first release was obtained.
Soviet official to whom large communities sent telegrams asking for the Rebbe's freedom and guaranteeing he had not sinned against the state.
Russian Czar whose story the Rebbe told at the Purim feast. As a prince, he was banished for two years by his father Czar Paul for spending without permission.
Father of Nikolai I who banished his son for two years as punishment for unauthorized spending during military maneuvers.
Soviet official who said at general assemblies in Moscow that the Yevsektzia is 'a thorn in the flesh of the party' and 'a blemish in the eye of the party.'

places.description: One of the most prominent streets in Leningrad where the Rebbe's dwelling was located on the corner with Fantilamanskaya 12. In Czarist times, it was an exclusive area where no Jew was allowed.
The other prominent street forming the corner where the Rebbe's dwelling was located. The dwelling had seven windows looking onto this street.
Major Soviet city where the Rebbe lived from 1924-1927. Location of his arrest and various community activities.
Traditional seat of Chabad-Lubavitch. The prayer room in the Rebbe's Leningrad dwelling was compared to the second hall in Lubavitch.
Location of the great rabbinic conference where the Rebbe was chosen as Honorary Chairman in absentia, which contributed to his arrest.
Province where Karastien was located and where the Rebbe sent secret representatives to establish chadarim despite strong Yevsektzia presence.
Resort town where Rabbi Katzenelenbogen was vacationing when the Rebbe sent delegations to dissuade him.
Notorious prison in Leningrad where the Rebbe was initially taken after his arrest.
City where the Rebbe was exiled for three years after his sentence was reduced from ten years on the Solovaki Islands.
Prison islands where the Rebbe was initially sentenced to ten years of hard labor before the sentence was reduced.
Capital city where the Rebbe's son-in-law went to function through the community there, and where various efforts were made for the Rebbe's release.
City where one of the synagogues was desecrated by the Yevsektzia who placed a pig in the Holy Ark with 'Paschal Sacrifice' written on it.
Region where the Baron from Kass served as Governor before being forced to leave his dwelling to the Rebbe.
Location of Ohalim (graves) where pidyonot were to be sent after the Rebbe's arrest.
Location of Ohalim (graves) where pidyonot were to be sent after the Rebbe's arrest.
Location of Ohalim (graves) where pidyonot were to be sent after the Rebbe's arrest.
City from where scholars came to aid in efforts for the Rebbe's release.
One of the large communities that sent telegrams for the Rebbe's freedom.
City where protests were sent from America regarding the Rebbe's arrest.
One of the large American cities from which telegrams arrived about the Rebbe's arrest.
Country from which news of the arrest spread and protests were organized.
Region where many of the Rebbe's emissaries were exiled after being arrested by the Yevsektzia.
Resort town where the strategy meeting was held at Rabbi Katzenelenbogen's home.

events.title_en: Baron from Kass signs over dwelling to Rebbe
events.description: The Baron from Kass, Governor of Besravia region, signed over the dwelling at Machovaya 22 and much of its furniture to the Rebbe in his own handwriting before being compelled to leave Leningrad.
events.passage: In Sivan 5684 (1924) the Baron was compelled, according to Soviet law, to leave Leningrad and was banished to one of the remote 'cities of exile.' Until this day many of the Baron's furnishings remain in the Rebbe's dwelling.

events.title_en: Discovery of theft at Opravlenia Doma
events.description: It was revealed that about 4,000 gold rubles were stolen from the Opravlenia Doma. The bookkeeper blamed the Oprovdom and tried to implicate the Rebbe.
events.passage: In January, 1927, it was 'revealed' that the Oprovdom manager of the building, had stolen a large sum, about 4,000 gold rubles, from the Opravlenia Doma.

events.title_en: Investigation of theft clears Rebbe's name
events.description: The chairman conducted a thorough investigation that proved the Rebbe's innocence and revealed the bookkeeper as the actual thief.
events.passage: During one of the long winter nights, we sat in his home—he, the bookkeeper, two people from the investigation committee, the manager from the Rebbe's house, and myself. Patiently, we sat and pored over the calculations until the morning, scrutinizing the accounts from the entire three years. It was obvious the Rebbe and his followers were honest and that the accusation was groundless.

events.description: Great conference of one hundred rabbis in Vohynnia-Padolia province where the Rebbe was chosen as Honorary Chairman despite not attending. This became a major cause of the Rebbe's arrest.
events.passage: The great conference in Karastien in the province of Vohynnia-Padolia was a major cause of the Rebbe's arrest. One hundred rabbis had gathered, and at the opening, with much fanfare, the Rebbe was chosen as the Honorary Chairman, notwithstanding the fact that he was not even in attendance.

events.title_en: Rebbe's telegram read at Karastien Conference
events.description: The Rebbe's reply telegram was read at the conference by Rabbi Zamsky while the entire assemblage stood up, greatly angering the Yevsektzia.
events.passage: When the Rebbe's reply was received in the middle of the conference, the entire assemblage stood up while the telegram was read by Rabbi Zamsky. This greatly angered the Yevsektzia.

events.description: The Rebbe sent secret representatives to establish chadarim in every city in Vohynnia-Padolia region, paying for them anonymously and forming hundreds of study groups.
events.passage: In the years 5685-5687 (1925-1927), he was informed of the eradication of Judaism in these places and of the general abandonment of religion, with the result that many Jewish towns were left without a children's teacher... Despite all this, the Rebbe sent secret special representatives and directed them to institute a cheder in every city in that area, large and small.

events.description: The Yevsektzia arrested many of the Rebbe's emissaries and had them exiled to Siberian towns, bringing their educational work to a halt.
events.passage: To our great despair, however, the Yevsektzia also learned of these developments and had many of the emissaries arrested, so that in 5687 their work was brought to a halt. They were exiled to the towns of Siberia, may G-d have mercy on them and deliver them from captivity to freedom.

events.description: Unprecedented Purim feast where the Rebbe spoke openly and intensely against the Yevsektzia, pounded his chest, and told Elye Chaim to write harsh letters. He also told the story of Nikolai and confronted the G.P.U. agents present.
events.passage: Purim of last year was totally without precedent. What we saw and heard during the Purim festive meal was unlike anything we had ever seen or heard in our entire lives; nothing comparable to this had been witnessed by our fathers or our fathers' fathers.

events.title_en: Anonymous article about Rebbe in Diska Pravda
events.description: After Passover, the Leningrad Diska Pravda newspaper printed an anonymous article about the 'famous tzaddik Schneersohn' settling at Machovaya Street, seen as a warning of what was to come.
events.passage: After Passover the Leningrad Diska Pravda newspaper unexpectedly printed the following anonymous article: The famous tzaddik Schneersohn from Lubavitch recently arrived in Leningrad and settled at Machovaya Street, number 22.

events.description: The Rebbe sent three rabbis along with his son-in-law Rabbi Gourary to Rabbi Katzenelenbogen to try to dissuade him from the General Assembly, but he refused to heed their words.
events.passage: He therefore composed a letter. Before circulating it, he again sent three rabbis along with his son-in-law Rabbi Gourary, to Rabbi Katzenelenbogen, who was vacationing in Tzara Sela, to sway him from his skewed path.

events.description: On the night of 15 Sivan, G.P.U. agents came to arrest the Rebbe. He calmly finished his dinner, requested coffee and mayim achronim, and was taken away in an automobile to Spalerka prison.
events.passage: On the night of the 15th of Sivan, the police came to arrest the Rebbe. The Rebbe, who was having his dinner, did not stand up, but quietly requested a cup of coffee. Then he requested mayim achronim, said the after-blessing, and went into his office where he sat, again calmly.

events.description: G.P.U. agents conducted a search of the Rebbe's office, treating him respectfully and even returning his father's manuscripts when he requested it.
events.passage: In general, the G.P.U. agents treated the Rebbe in a respectful manner. They did not speak to him over harshly; nor did they make light of his honor. The search was relatively lenient. For example, they were about to take the manuscripts of the Rebbe's father of blessed memory when the Rebbe asked them to desist, and the manuscripts were immediately returned.

events.description: On Shabbat, the chassidim received the first receipt signed by the Rebbe from prison, bringing overwhelming joy and being passed from hand to hand among hundreds of people.
events.passage: On Shabbat, 25 Sivan (June 25), we obtained the first receipt the Rebbe had signed. Our joy that day was overwhelming. The receipt was passed from hand to hand among hundreds of people, who regarded it as miraculous.

events.description: An anxious girl called to inform that the Rebbe was to be exiled to Solovaki Islands for ten years of hard labor, but the family could see him before departure.
events.passage: On Wednesday, Erev Rosh Chodesh Tammuz (June 29), at ten o'clock in the morning, the telephone rang... 'Why are you sitting at home and not going to see Rabbi Schneersohn?' she demanded. 'Prepare him food for the way immediately, and come to see him! The entire family should immediately come to Spalerka to see him, for today he will be exiled to the Solovaki Islands for ten years of hard labor.'

events.description: The Rebbe's sentence was reduced from ten years on Solovaki Islands to three years exile in Kostroma, and he was allowed to stop at home for several hours on the way.
events.passage: The sentence was reduced to three years exile in Kostroma. On his way to Kostroma, the Rebbe was allowed to stop at home for several hours.

institutions.description: Soviet executive commission that would summarily reject all applications for official prayer assembly permits.
Soviet housing authority that managed residential buildings and required permits for prayer assemblies by contract.
Soviet secret police organization that conducted arrests and investigations. Agents were stationed to monitor the Rebbe's activities.
Soviet secret police organization, predecessor to G.P.U. The chairman/director was formerly prominent among them.
Jewish section of the Communist Party that persecuted religious Jews, confiscated synagogues, and opposed Jewish education. Described as 'a thorn in the flesh of the party.'
Yevsektzia institution in Leningrad created to spread Yevsektzia literature. Experienced sharp decline in membership and morale by 1927.
Yevsektzia newspaper that had sold thousands of subscriptions but was slashed to fewer than one thousand in Leningrad by 1927.
Soviet judicial body that handled serious criminal cases. Received reports about the theft investigation.
Committee formed by the Rebbe in Moscow to which teachers could report difficulties from the Yevsektzia or other mishaps.

communities.description: Chassidic community that gathered around the Rebbe in Leningrad from 1924-1927, holding regular prayers and study sessions despite lack of official permits.

concepts.definition_short: Ritual washing of hands performed after eating bread and before reciting Grace After Meals
Redemption prayer or charitable contribution made at gravesites of righteous individuals for spiritual intercession
Gravesites or memorial structures over the graves of righteous individuals, particularly Rebbes

teachings.text_english: Thank you for the honor you have bestowed upon me. With this you demonstrate that you are proceeding in the ways of Rabbi Schneersohn of Lubavitch and Rabbi Chaim of Brisk, loyal to the ideals of our unforgettable teachers. The entire Jewish nation is anticipating that you will continue in your ways without compromise. Wish the participants well. Best wishes for success.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe dispatched a reply: Thank you for the honor you have bestowed upon me...

teachings.text_english: Elye Chaim, Elye Chaim! I told you to write harshly last year, but you did not listen, and this is why there has been so much suffering all year. After Shabbat you shall write a letter to all the cities and villages with these words: 'We possessed a Rebbe, and he left us his son to guide us, and the son has instructed us to write in his name that anyone handing his child over to the school of the Yevsektzia will be severely punished by Heaven, Heaven forbid.' Will you write this? Remember well what I say to you!
teachings.passage: In the midst of the Purim feast, the Rebbe abruptly stood up, pulled at his shirt to reveal his heart, and pounded hard with his fist on his bare flesh, calling out...

teachings.text_english: When you see the body consumed, have no feelings of pity. Concern yourself solely with protecting the head.
teachings.passage: He called me again, and with a radiant countenance declared, 'When you see the body consumed, have no feelings of pity. Concern yourself solely with protecting the head.'

teachings.text_english: I asked my father, 'in the manner of Nikolai?' And he replied, 'like Nikolai!'
teachings.passage: The Rebbe answered, 'I asked my father, in the manner of Nikolai?' And he replied, 'like Nikolai!'

teachings.text_english: Yemach shemam! [May their names be blotted out!] I know that they are here; I am not afraid of them.
teachings.passage: We begged the Rebbe to stop, but he faced them directly and cried out: 'Yemach shemam! [May their names be blotted out!] I know that they are here; I am not afraid of them.'

teachings.text_english: Mother, please return to your room. Say Psalms and weep in prayer to G-d; that will help.
teachings.passage: Before she could speak, the Rebbe turned to her with great deference and said, 'Mother, please return to your room. Say Psalms and weep in prayer to G-d; that will help.'

teachings.text_english: I do nothing on my own. I consulted with Father.
teachings.passage: After these harrowing moments the Rebbe spoke, 'I do nothing on my own. I consulted with Father.'

teachings.text_english: Zalman, if they should ever make a great bonfire and confront you with the choice of yielding your child to their schools or casting yourself into the flames, do you know what should be done? I am telling you, Zalman: let yourself be cast into the flames to prevent your children from being given over to their schools!
teachings.passage: 'Zalman,' he said, 'if they should ever make a great bonfire and confront you with the choice of yielding your child to their schools or casting yourself into the flames, do you know what should be done? I am telling you, Zalman: let yourself be cast into the flames to prevent your children from being given over to their schools!'

teachings.text_english: Think very hard whether you will gain from this—won't you lose from the great uproar my arrest will create?
teachings.passage: After being informed of his situation, he told them, 'Think very hard whether you will gain from this—won't you lose from the great uproar my arrest will create?'

teachings.text_english: Nevertheless, I request that you repeat my words by telephone to your superior and consult with him. Perhaps you will change it to a house arrest.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe answered: 'Nevertheless, I request that you repeat my words by telephone to your superior and consult with him. Perhaps you will change it to a house arrest.'

teachings.text_english: True, your father had the privilege of serving my father of blessed memory; your father followed in the ways of my father. But you want me to proceed in your way. You did not, and never will, merit this. I will not go in your ways.
teachings.passage: He took the package from the Rebbe's hand, but the Rebbe told the aide to take it back from him. 'True,' he answered, 'your father had the privilege of serving my father of blessed memory; your father followed in the ways of my father. But you want me to proceed in your way. You did not, and never will, merit this. I will not go in your ways.'

teachings.text_english: First you must send pidyonot to the Ohalim in Haditch, Niezin, Lubavitch and Rostov, not via telegraph, and not via special messenger, but through special express post.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe answered, 'First you must send pidyonot to the Ohalim in Haditch, Niezin, Lubavitch and Rostov, not via telegraph, and not via special messenger, but through special express post.'

teachings.text_english: When I saw you from the automobile I was at peace, knowing that a friendly person would be in the house. And I recalled how we saw one another twelve hours ago, and under what circumstances we see each other now.
teachings.passage: Later, in Kostroma, the Rebbe told me: 'When I saw you from the automobile I was at peace, knowing that a friendly person would be in the house. And I recalled how we saw one another twelve hours ago, and under what circumstances we see each other now.'

teachings.text_english: Master of the Universe! It was for Your Torah and Your service that my son was taken! Help me, dear G-d, and save me from these enemies! 'May G-d be with us as he was with our holy ancestors, to not abandon nor forsake us.'
teachings.passage: The first sight I beheld was the old Rebbetzin standing on a bench next to the Holy Ark, the top half of her body bent within, and calling out bitterly, 'Master of the Universe! It was for Your Torah and Your service that my son was taken! Help me, dear G-d, and save me from these enemies!'
