teachings.text_english: Blessed are You, L-rd our G-d, King of the Universe, who has granted us life, sustained us, and enabled us to reach this occasion.
teachings.passage: This blessing is recited when purchasing something of value, reaching a milestone in life, or eating a new fruit

teachings.text_english: You should know what you are getting into.
teachings.passage: His father warned him that opening such a yeshivah would be complicated.

teachings.text_english: For many years, I have been considering establishing a yeshivah for scholars of Torah who would dedicate themselves to the study of chasidic philosophy in addition to the classic study of Talmud. There were obstacles that stood in the way, but now is the time to create the yeshivah.
teachings.passage: He told those assembled about establishing the yeshiva

teachings.text_english: Establishing this institution will demand much from us, both materially and spiritually – the dedication of our hearts and souls. I am deeply apprehensive and pained by the frozen and parched spiritual level of religious Jews. Gone is the fire and excitement in doing mitzvot; their actions are dry and frozen, and everything is done without feeling.
teachings.passage: He addressed the crowd about establishing the yeshiva

teachings.text_english: We need to remedy this. The teaching of Chasidut is a complete Torah that must be studied in depth and would enrich all aspects of Jewish learning. The new yeshivah would infuse in the students spiritual values and instill in them a sensitivity to godliness.
teachings.passage: The Rashab explained the purpose of studying Chasidism

teachings.text_english: With divine blessing, I have established a yeshivah in which both nigleh [the revealed part of Torah, i.e., Talmud] and Chasidism are studied. They should understand Chasidut the same way that they understand a concept in the revealed Torah. The One who gave the Torah should assist the students of the yeshivah.
teachings.passage: During hakafot, the Rashab announced the yeshiva's establishment

teachings.text_english: The yeshivah that was established by the grace of G-d doesn't have a name. Now I am naming it Tomchei Temimim [literally, 'Supporters of the Sincere'], and the title of those who learn and conduct themselves in its spirit will be temimim [sincere, virtuous, complete ones; tamim in the singular].
teachings.passage: The Rashab named the yeshiva during Simchat Torah

teachings.text_english: I entrusted to you the directorship of the yeshivah, making you responsible for both its spiritual and material welfare. Our vineyard, Yeshivas Tomchei Temimim, is now three years old, and it has produced fine fruits. Temimim, you are like the soldiers of King David. You should divorce yourself from worldly concerns and make scholarship and spiritual development your priority.
teachings.passage: Three years after the yeshiva's founding, the Rashab laid out a mission statement

teachings.text_english: When your wellsprings [chasidic teachings] will be disseminated outward.
teachings.passage: The Messiah answered when asked when he would redeem the world

teachings.text_english: The dark clouds that will last at least twenty-two years. A country that persecutes Judaism will eventually fall. The Jewish group that combats Judaism will be broken and their leaders killed. Lenin will die in a strange fashion. Trotsky will be exiled, and they will execute him. Stalin will follow the spirit of the time as needed, and he will be adorned with epaulets.
teachings.passage: The Rashab summoned his son to discuss what he believed would be a stormy future for the Jews in Russia

teachings.text_english: Yosef Yitzchak, for the sake of strengthening Torah, reverence of Heaven, and support for Judaism, you will have to act with actual mesirat nefesh (self-sacrifice), risking your life. When it becomes difficult, remember my instructions to you that you must be willing to sacrifice your life to spread Torah, reverence of Heaven, and support for Judaism.
teachings.passage: That night in 1920, the Rashab entrusted his son with the sacred mission

teachings.text_english: Why are you crying? Once the month of Adar begins, we increase joy!
teachings.passage: Hearing these terrifying words, the Rayatz began to cry. His father consoled him.

teachings.text_english: On Simchat Torah we danced and sang and now we will also. And if it's a [divine] test, we will persevere. Yosef Yitzchak, don't worry. We're going to be completely fine.
teachings.passage: The Rashab encouraged singing despite concerns about attracting police attention

teachings.text_english: I have no fear of them. We have to say chasidut, and they will be batul (nullified, as if they don't exist).
teachings.passage: When police officers entered the room during the farbrengen

teachings.text_english: G-d should help that we should be close spiritually.
teachings.passage: The Rashab's cryptic response about celebrating next year in Lubavitch

teachings.text_english: I am going to heaven, and I am leaving you my manuscripts. Take me in one and we will be together.
teachings.passage: The Rashab asked to be moved into his study and spoke these shocking words

teachings.text_english: Hitpaalut? Hitpaalut? Mochin, mochin – Excitement? Excitement? Intellect, intellect.
teachings.passage: He was referring to the chasidic teaching that one's intellect should control his emotions

teachings.text_english: Gevald, gevald, where was my understanding of the situation? The whole winter, he clearly warned me that he would pass away, and I did not comprehend it.
teachings.passage: Late Sunday evening, the Rayatz confided in Berel Rivkin

teachings.text_english: My father instructed me to teach chasidic discourses to the community. But who am I to do this? How can I stand in front of anash? I can fulfill that directive by delivering the maamar to you.

teachings.text_english: After some time, I opened the will. My eyes darkened when I read that the leadership of the community was placed on me. For many days I was overwhelmed; I made a step forward and then took a step back. I became deeply ill, but G-d with His boundless kindness restored me to life, and like a humbled servant, it is incumbent on me to follow my father's sacred instructions.
teachings.passage: The Rayatz revealed his reaction to reading his father's instructions

teachings.text_english: I don't know which Schneersohn you seek. If you enter someone's home, surely you know in advance who dwells there. This drama is pointless. Deliver your message and clearly state your wishes.
teachings.passage: Facing the armed men, the Rebbe remained composed, unimpressed by the show of force

teachings.text_english: We are members of our father's party, apolitical Jewish women who hold dear Jewish traditions and despise new trends.
teachings.passage: They replied unabashedly to Nachmanson's question about party membership

teachings.text_english: The entire tragedy is that you wish to accomplish everything by power and coercion, attempting to intimidate the intelligent and informed with the power of the fist and the threats of the gun.
teachings.passage: Sheina retorted to Nachmanson's intimidation

teachings.text_english: I believe that the arrest will result in highly negative publicity, and you should proceed with caution until the truth is clarified – if you are actually seeking the truth. However, if you mean to conceal this error or libel with lies and falsehood, I am certain that you will regret it.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe refused to sign form and warned officers

teachings.text_english: Only wishful thinking could imagine that pleas and tears could help.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe told his daughter after her pleas

teachings.text_english: Your grandfather was a Chasid of my grandfather, so he was privileged to carry those bags to the place of my grandfather's choosing. You, however, desire to carry it so that I should go to a destination of your choosing. This is not happening. I will not follow your way.
teachings.passage: Response to Lulov's offer to carry the Rebbe's bag

teachings.text_english: And what of G-d? Who has done this? Who generated this entire sequence of events? Everything has its source in G-d. True, I am indeed a son, a husband, a father, a father-in-law, one who loves and is beloved. They are all dependent on me, but I – and they in turn – are dependent on G-d, who spoke and created the world.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe's thoughts while sitting on bench in prison

teachings.text_english: Power and coercion are alien to the chasidic path. Among Chasidim, leadership implies spiritual stature that aspires to attain moral and spiritual excellence and to inspire others to follow that same path.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe responded to accusations during interrogation

teachings.text_english: I have no regrets. You can threaten me with serious punishments, but they will not influence my decision.
teachings.passage: Rebbe's rejection of Lulov's demand to support conference

teachings.text_english: This, all the nations of the world must know: Only our bodies were sent into exile and subjugated to alien rule; our souls were not given over into captivity and foreign rule. We must proclaim openly and before all that any matter affecting the Jewish religion, the Torah and its mitzvot and customs, is not subject to the coercion of others.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe rose on platform and gave defiant speech

teachings.text_english: Don't fear anyone. Each of you should seek to influence those in your community. As the Talmud says, a person who teaches Torah to a child is considered the child's own parent.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe urged his Chasidim during final holiday

teachings.text_english: Whoever wants a bond with me will not make any compromises. It will be very difficult, but that is the condition.
teachings.passage: The Rebbe's last words to Chasidim

teachings.text_english: There will be difficult times; you will need to have mesirat nefesh bepoel (actual self-sacrifice). But eventually, the Communist government will disintegrate.
teachings.passage: Prediction recorded in diary from 1926

teachings.text_english: Generally speaking, even regarding a private person, his wedding constitutes an all-encompassing event. For me, however, my wedding day brought me into more general and communal affairs. The wedding connected me to you, and you to me

Some of the Jews who visit me in my office were brought up in religious environments, but sadly they didn't keep up their observance once they came to America. Some of the elder Chasidim have chastised me for not criticizing their behavior, instead warmly welcoming everyone equally

G-d created a feeling of unlimited love in a father and mother, no matter how many children they have. And if one of the children is missing something, like a hand or a leg, the parent will naturally feel a more special, unique love for that child. G-d, too, loves every Jew completely, but if a Jew is missing something, He loves him or her even more. So if a Jew comes into your office and does not observe the commandments, we must accept him wholeheartedly; this is a precious Jew. When you greet someone who does not lay tefillin – missing a hand, so to speak – or who does not attend synagogue – missing a leg, so to speak – you must have a deeper love for him, because the more he is lacking, the greater your love must be. You should conduct yourself like G-d does
